text:
Question: Why is my NTFS disk suddenly read-only on macOS?

Posted by alice_dev | 2024-03-15 | Score: 12 | Views: 3401

I have been using this 2TB disk for years without permission issues.
Open Disk Utilities shows Repair Disk Permissions disabled.

Reply by bob_helper | Score: 45 | Accepted Answer
You are getting read-only because NTFS is natively read-only on macOS unless you install Tuxera NTFS or similar third-party driver.

Nested reply by alice_dev | Score: 3
thank you for this response, your answer gave me a hint so it should be accepted.

Reply by troll99 | Score: -2
fake news lol

Signature: bob_helper — Senior Sysadmin | Contact: bob@example.com
actual_output:
I have been using this 2TB disk for years without permission issues.
Open Disk Utilities shows Repair Disk Permissions disabled.
You are getting read-only because NTFS is natively read-only on macOS unless you install Tuxera NTFS or similar third-party driver.

text:
How do I fix CUDA out of memory during training?

> I tried reducing batch size but still OOM on 24GB card.

Try gradient checkpointing and mixed precision fp16. Also enable expandable_segments:True in PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF.

User456: +1 this worked for me
User789: propaganda
actual_output:
How do I fix CUDA out of memory during training?
I tried reducing batch size but still OOM on 24GB card.
Try gradient checkpointing and mixed precision fp16. Also enable expandable_segments:True in PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF.
Try gradient checkpointing and mixed precision fp16. Also enable expandable_segments:True in PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF.
